_concept: An imagined 'lost' track from a 2008 French dream-pop record obsessed with teenage nostalgia and 1980s John-Hughes-movie melancholy. The album's whole world is the last summer before everyone scatters: bikes at dusk, public-pool chlorine, a tape deck in a parked car, fireworks no one wanted to end. This track is the one that didn't make the cut because it was 'too sad to close side B.' It's a slow-burn shoegaze swell that breaks open into an euphoric, gated-reverb chorus. Per the counterpoint rule: the lyrics are a quiet goodbye, but the music is huge and radiant — the sadness is dressed as a celebration. A teenage spoken-word bridge (a hallmark of the album) names the feeling out loud.
title: Pool Lights, 4 A.M.
tags: 1980s-inspired French dream pop, dreamy shoegaze, lush layered analog synthesizers, big gated reverb drums, shimmering chorus-laden electric guitars, breathy reverb-soaked male and female vocals, wall-of-sound wash, mid-tempo build into euphoric soaring chorus, nostalgic teenage coming-of-age atmosphere, warm cassette-tape saturation, cinematic John Hughes end-credits feeling, wistful but radiant, spoken-word teenage bridge, saxophone swell in the outro
negative_tags: aggressive, heavy metal, trap, rap, harsh distortion, lo-fi bedroom, autotune, EDM drop
prompt:
[Intro]
(soft synth pad, distant reverb, a single shimmering guitar)

[Verse 1]
We left the porch light on for no one
The street was ours and then it wasn't
You wrote our names in the fog of the glass
I knew already this wouldn't last

[Pre-Chorus]
And the radio plays a song we'll forget
But not tonight, not tonight just yet

[Chorus]
Under the pool lights, four in the morning
Your hair full of summer, the sky going golden
Hold on, hold on, don't let it end
We were never this young again
Under the pool lights, four in the morning

[Verse 2]
The cassette deck warm in the dashboard glow
You said you'd remember, you said you'd let go
The fireworks died but the smoke stayed low
Like everything beautiful, slow, so slow

[Pre-Chorus]
And the radio plays a song we'll forget
But not tonight, not tonight just yet

[Chorus]
Under the pool lights, four in the morning
Your hair full of summer, the sky going golden
Hold on, hold on, don't let it end
We were never this young again
Under the pool lights, four in the morning

[Bridge - spoken, teenage voice, breathless over swelling synths]
I think about that summer more than I should.
We thought the night would just keep going.
We thought we'd stay exactly like that forever —
wet hair, no curfew, the whole town asleep but us.
Nobody tells you it's the last time while it's happening.
You only find out later, when you're driving past the pool
and the gate is locked and the lights are off
and you're not seventeen anymore.

[Final Chorus - bigger, doubled vocals, gated drums full]
Under the pool lights, four in the morning
Your hair full of summer, the sky going golden
Hold on, hold on, don't let it end
We were never this young again
(We were never, we were never)
Under the pool lights, four in the morning

[Outro]
(saxophone swell, the wall of sound widens and slowly fades into tape hiss)
Don't let it end... don't let it end...
